 {"id":"bd-17zi","title":"EPIC: Fallback Release Infrastructure (ACFS)","description":"# Agent-Controlled Fallback System (ACFS) for Multi-Tool Releases

## Background & Problem Statement

GitHub Actions is limiting usage due to high volume (~$5k/month equivalent). When CI/CD jobs stay queued for >10 minutes, it indicates throttling. We need an automated fallback system that:

1. **Detects throttling** - Monitors GH Actions queue time, triggers fallback when jobs sit >10 min
2. **Builds locally** - Linux on Contabo superserver (css), macOS on Mac mini (mmini)
3. **Handles Windows** - Cross-compilation or other strategies TBD
4. **Uploads releases** - Manually create releases and upload binaries when throttled
5. **Smart installers** - curl|bash one-liners that try release first, build from source if stale

## Infrastructure Available

| Host | Alias | Purpose | Connection |
|------|-------|---------|------------|
| Contabo Superserver | css | Linux x86_64 builds | ssh css |
| Mac mini | mmini | macOS ARM64/x86_64 builds | ssh mmini |
| OVH servers | yto, fmd | Backup build capacity | ssh yto/fmd |

## Tools Affected (16 total)

cass, bv, cm, ubs, xf, ru, slb, caam, ntm, br, dcg, ms, wa, pt, rch, mcp_agent_mail

## Languages & Toolchains

- **Rust** (majority): cargo, rustup, cross-compilation via cargo-zigbuild or cross
- **Go**: go build, goreleaser
- **TypeScript/Bun**: bun build, bun compile

## Success Criteria

1. Any tool can be released within 30 minutes even when GH Actions is throttled
2. curl-bash installers work on Linux/macOS/Windows without manual intervention
3. Installers auto-detect stale releases (>10 commits behind) and offer source builds
4. Toolchain installation is automatic and non-destructive
5. All paths have unit tests and E2E validation scripts with detailed logging

## Key Design Decisions

1. **10-minute threshold**: Chosen as balance between patience and productivity
2. **Try release first**: Network download is faster than compilation for most users
3. **Commit-based staleness**: More accurate than date-based (active development = fresh releases)
4. **Non-destructive toolchains**: Never break existing installations
5. **Detailed logging**: Every step logged for debugging failed installs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:06:01.393302769Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T13:06:01.393302769Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-n11g","title":"Fix one-liner installer + flock dependency","description":"## Problem\nThe installer run via: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/repo_updater/main/install.sh | bash\ncan fail with: 'Could not parse version from GitHub API response' (cached older installer / GitHub API rate limits). Also ru parallel sync currently uses flock for atomic writes, which is missing on some platforms (notably macOS).\n\n## Goals\n- Installer should not require GitHub API to install latest; use releases/latest/download assets and cache-bust internal downloads.\n- Ensure installer docs/behavior clearly support cache-busting (ru_cb query param).\n- Remove hard runtime dependency on system flock by using a portable lock fallback (mkdir/ln lock), or otherwise ensure reliable cross-platform behavior without requiring system package installs.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- One-liner installer works reliably without GitHub API.\n- Installer uses cache-busting for its own downloads and provides actionable guidance.\n- Parallel sync atomic results aggregation works on macOS and Linux without requiring flock.\n- ShellCheck passes (severity warning+).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-05T18:46:06.325794458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T19:06:33.033656291Z","closed_at":"2026-01-05T19:06:33.033656291Z","close_reason":"Core fix complete: installer avoids GitHub API, flock replaced with portable dir-based locking. Tests need updating for new lock function names.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"bd-n16r","title":"Audit slb (Simultaneous Launch Button) for package distribution","description":"# Audit: slb (Simultaneous Launch Button) for Package Distribution

## Tool Overview

**Repository**: /data/projects/slb (https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/simultaneous_launch_button)
**Language**: Go (assumed based on "Coming Soon via GoReleaser" note)
**Purpose**: Two-person rule enforcement for dangerous commands

slb implements the "two-person rule" (also called "four-eyes principle") for executing dangerous commands:
- Requires two separate confirmations before executing high-risk operations
- Prevents accidental execution of destructive commands
- Provides audit trail of who authorized what
- Useful for production deployments, database operations, etc.

## Audit Checklist

### 1. Binary Distribution Readiness
- [ ] Verify project language (Go expected)
- [ ] Check for existing GitHub releases
- [ ] Check if releases include pre-built binaries

### 2. GoReleaser Status
- [ ] Check for existing .goreleaser.yml
- [ ] If missing, flag for GoReleaser setup

### 3. CLI Interface
- [ ] Verify CLI interface
- [ ] Check for --version, --help flags
- [ ] Document command syntax

### 4. Security Model
- [ ] Document how two-person rule is enforced
- [ ] Check for network/communication requirements
- [ ] Note any authentication mechanisms

## Platform Considerations

Security tooling should be available everywhere:
- macOS/Linux: Primary use case (server ops, deployments)
- Windows: Less common for ops but still valuable

## Related Tool

This tool may relate to destructive_command_guard, which also provides safety rails for dangerous commands. Document the relationship/differences:
- slb: Two-person rule (collaborative confirmation)
- destructive_command_guard: Single-user confirmation with cooldowns

## Use Case Examples

1. Production deployment gates requiring dual approval
2. Database migration commands needing verification
3. Kubernetes namespace operations requiring sign-off

## Expected Outcome

Determine packaging readiness and document any special considerations for the two-person authentication flow.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-14T03:23:48.934218318Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T04:15:13.601471019Z","closed_at":"2026-01-14T04:15:13.601471019Z","close_reason":"AUDIT COMPLETE: slb (Simultaneous Launch Button) package distribution partially set up.

FINDINGS:
✓ GoReleaser: Comprehensive config with Homebrew brews, Scoop scoops, nfpms (deb/rpm/apk), SBOM, and cosign signing
✓ Release workflow: .github/workflows/release.yml with full CI/CD pipeline
✓ Releases: v0.1.0 (Dec 25, 2025) with all artifacts (binaries, packages, SBOMs, signatures)
✓ Secrets: Added HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN (was missing)

REMAINING:
- No Homebrew formula yet (next release will create it)
- No Scoop manifest yet (next release will create it)
- README may need Homebrew/Scoop install instructions

ACTION: Next release (v0.1.1+) will auto-publish to homebrew-tap and scoop-bucket.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
-{"id":"bd-n3fc","title":"Create Installation Verification Scripts with Detailed Logging","description":"# Create Installation Verification Scripts with Detailed Logging

## Background

Currently, there is no automated way to verify that:
1. Formula/manifest installations actually work
2. Installed tools run correctly (`--version` returns expected output)
3. Upgrades work properly
4. Uninstallation is clean

We need comprehensive installation verification scripts that can run:
- Locally (for development testing)
- In CI (GitHub Actions on multiple platforms)
- As part of E2E testing

## Goals

Create scripts that thoroughly test the user experience of installing tools from our package repositories, with detailed logging to diagnose any failures.

## Script 1: Homebrew Installation Verifier

Location: `/data/projects/homebrew-tap/scripts/verify-installation.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Installation verification script with structured logging
# Usage: ./verify-installation.sh [--json] [--verbose] [TOOL...]

source "$(dirname "$0")/test-helpers.sh"

# Default to all tools if none specified
TOOLS="${@:-cass xf cm ru ubs}"
RESULTS=()
FAILED=0

verify_tool() {
    local tool="$1"
    local start_time=$(date +%s%3N)
    
    log "info" "Starting verification" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "start"
    
    # Phase 1: Check formula exists
    log "info" "Checking formula availability" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "formula_check"
    if ! brew info "dicklesworthstone/tap/$tool" &>/dev/null; then
        log "error" "Formula not found" "tool" "$tool" "error_code" "FORMULA_NOT_FOUND"
        return 1
    fi
    log "info" "Formula found" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "formula_check" "result" "pass"
    
    # Phase 2: Install
    log "info" "Installing tool" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "install"
    local install_output install_exit
    install_output=$(brew install "dicklesworthstone/tap/$tool" 2>&1) 
    install_exit=$?
    
    if [[ $install_exit -ne 0 ]]; then
        log "error" "Installation failed" "tool" "$tool" "exit_code" "$install_exit" \
            "output" "${install_output:0:500}" "phase" "install"
        return 1
    fi
    log "info" "Installation succeeded" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "install" "result" "pass"
    
    # Phase 3: Version check
    log "info" "Checking version command" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "version_check"
    local version_output version_exit
    version_output=$("$tool" --version 2>&1) || version_exit=$?
    version_exit=${version_exit:-0}
    
    if [[ $version_exit -ne 0 ]]; then
        log "error" "Version command failed" "tool" "$tool" "exit_code" "$version_exit" \
            "output" "$version_output" "phase" "version_check"
        # Dont fail entirely, tool might not support --version
        log "warn" "Tool may not support --version flag" "tool" "$tool"
    else
        # Extract version number
        local version=$(echo "$version_output" | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+')
        log "info" "Version check passed" "tool" "$tool" "version" "$version" \
            "output" "$version_output" "phase" "version_check" "result" "pass"
    fi
    
    # Phase 4: Basic functionality test
    log "info" "Running basic functionality test" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "smoke_test"
    local smoke_result smoke_exit
    case "$tool" in
        cass)
            smoke_result=$(cass health 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
        xf)
            smoke_result=$(xf --help 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
        cm)
            smoke_result=$(cm status 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
        ru)
            smoke_result=$(ru --help 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
        ubs)
            smoke_result=$(ubs --help 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
        *)
            smoke_result=$("$tool" --help 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
    esac
    smoke_exit=${smoke_exit:-0}
    
    if [[ $smoke_exit -ne 0 ]]; then
        log "warn" "Smoke test returned non-zero" "tool" "$tool" "exit_code" "$smoke_exit" \
            "output" "${smoke_result:0:300}" "phase" "smoke_test"
    else
        log "info" "Smoke test passed" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "smoke_test" "result" "pass"
    fi
    
    # Phase 5: Uninstall
    log "info" "Uninstalling tool" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "uninstall"
    local uninstall_output uninstall_exit
    uninstall_output=$(brew uninstall "$tool" 2>&1)
    uninstall_exit=$?
    
    if [[ $uninstall_exit -ne 0 ]]; then
        log "error" "Uninstall failed" "tool" "$tool" "exit_code" "$uninstall_exit" \
            "output" "$uninstall_output" "phase" "uninstall"
        return 1
    fi
    log "info" "Uninstall succeeded" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "uninstall" "result" "pass"
    
    # Phase 6: Verify clean uninstall
    log "info" "Verifying clean uninstall" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "cleanup_check"
    if command -v "$tool" &>/dev/null; then
        log "error" "Tool still in PATH after uninstall" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "cleanup_check"
        return 1
    fi
    log "info" "Clean uninstall verified" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "cleanup_check" "result" "pass"
    
    local end_time=$(date +%s%3N)
    local duration=$((end_time - start_time))
    
    log "info" "Verification complete" "tool" "$tool" "result" "pass" "duration_ms" "$duration"
    return 0
}

# Main execution
log "info" "Starting installation verification suite" "tools" "$TOOLS" "platform" "$(uname -s)"

for tool in $TOOLS; do
    if verify_tool "$tool"; then
        RESULTS+=("$tool:pass")
    else
        RESULTS+=("$tool:fail")
        ((FAILED++))
    fi
done

# Summary
log "info" "Verification suite complete" "total" "${#RESULTS[@]}" "failed" "$FAILED" \
    "results" "$(IFS=,; echo "${RESULTS[*]}")"

exit $FAILED
```

## Script 2: Scoop Installation Verifier

Location: `/data/projects/scoop-bucket/scripts/verify-installation.ps1` (PowerShell)
Location: `/data/projects/scoop-bucket/scripts/verify-installation.sh` (for CI)

The PowerShell version follows the same pattern but uses Scoop commands:
- `scoop install dicklesworthstone/<tool>`
- `<tool> --version`
- `scoop uninstall <tool>`

## Script 3: Cross-Platform CI Workflow

Location: `/data/projects/homebrew-tap/.github/workflows/verify-installation.yml`

```yaml
name: Verify Installation

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * 0'  # Weekly on Sunday
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      tools:
        description: 'Tools to test (comma-separated or "all")'
        default: 'all'

jobs:
  verify-macos-intel:
    runs-on: macos-13  # Intel
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install Homebrew tap
        run: brew tap dicklesworthstone/tap
      - name: Run verification
        run: |
          chmod +x scripts/verify-installation.sh
          LOG_FORMAT=json ./scripts/verify-installation.sh 2>&1 | tee results-macos-intel.json
      - name: Upload results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: results-macos-intel
          path: results-macos-intel.json

  verify-macos-arm:
    runs-on: macos-14  # ARM
    steps:
      # Same as above

  verify-linux:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install Homebrew
        run: |
          /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
          echo 'eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)"' >> ~/.bashrc
          eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
      - name: Install tap and verify
        run: |
          brew tap dicklesworthstone/tap
          LOG_FORMAT=json ./scripts/verify-installation.sh 2>&1 | tee results-linux.json

  verify-windows:
    runs-on: windows-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install Scoop
        run: |
          Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser -Force
          irm get.scoop.sh | iex
      - name: Add bucket and verify
        run: |
          scoop bucket add dicklesworthstone https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/scoop-bucket
          # Run PowerShell verification script
```

## Logging Output Format

All scripts produce JSON logs in this format:

```json
{"timestamp":"2026-01-13T22:30:00Z","level":"info","message":"Starting verification","tool":"cass","phase":"start","platform":"Darwin"}
{"timestamp":"2026-01-13T22:30:01Z","level":"info","message":"Checking formula availability","tool":"cass","phase":"formula_check"}
{"timestamp":"2026-01-13T22:30:02Z","level":"info","message":"Formula found","tool":"cass","phase":"formula_check","result":"pass"}
{"timestamp":"2026-01-13T22:30:03Z","level":"info","message":"Installing tool","tool":"cass","phase":"install"}
{"timestamp":"2026-01-13T22:30:15Z","level":"info","message":"Installation succeeded","tool":"cass","phase":"install","result":"pass","duration_ms":12000}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] verify-installation.sh for Homebrew created and tested
- [ ] verify-installation.ps1 for Scoop created and tested
- [ ] CI workflow runs on all platforms (macOS Intel, macOS ARM, Linux, Windows)
- [ ] JSON logging produces parseable output
- [ ] Scripts handle network failures gracefully
- [ ] Scripts provide clear error messages on failure
- [ ] Results uploaded as CI artifacts
- [ ] Weekly scheduled runs configured
- [ ] Manual dispatch available for testing

## Error Handling Requirements

Scripts must handle and log:
1. Network timeouts during install
2. Missing dependencies
3. Permission errors
4. Tool crashes during smoke test
5. Incomplete uninstallation

## Background

1. Formula/manifest installations actually work

We need comprehensive installation verification scripts that can run:

## Goals

## Script 1: Homebrew Installation Verifier

Location: `/data/projects/homebrew-tap/scripts/verify-installation.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Installation verification script with structured logging
# Usage: ./verify-installation.sh [--json] [--verbose] [TOOL...]

source "$(dirname "$0")/test-helpers.sh"

# Default to all tools if none specified
TOOLS="${@:-cass xf cm ru ubs}"
RESULTS=()
FAILED=0

verify_tool() {
    local tool="$1"
    local start_time=$(date +%s%3N)
    
    log "info" "Starting verification" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "start"
    
    # Phase 1: Check formula exists
    log "info" "Checking formula availability" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "formula_check"
    if ! brew info "dicklesworthstone/tap/$tool" &>/dev/null; then
        log "error" "Formula not found" "tool" "$tool" "error_code" "FORMULA_NOT_FOUND"
        return 1
    fi
    log "info" "Formula found" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "formula_check" "result" "pass"
    
    # Phase 2: Install
    log "info" "Installing tool" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "install"
    local install_output install_exit
    install_output=$(brew install "dicklesworthstone/tap/$tool" 2>&1) 
    install_exit=$?
    
    if [[ $install_exit -ne 0 ]]; then
        log "error" "Installation failed" "tool" "$tool" "exit_code" "$install_exit" \
            "output" "${install_output:0:500}" "phase" "install"
        return 1
    fi
    log "info" "Installation succeeded" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "install" "result" "pass"
    
    # Phase 3: Version check
    log "info" "Checking version command" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "version_check"
    local version_output version_exit
    version_output=$("$tool" --version 2>&1) || version_exit=$?
    version_exit=${version_exit:-0}
    
    if [[ $version_exit -ne 0 ]]; then
        log "error" "Version command failed" "tool" "$tool" "exit_code" "$version_exit" \
            "output" "$version_output" "phase" "version_check"
        # Dont fail entirely, tool might not support --version
        log "warn" "Tool may not support --version flag" "tool" "$tool"
    else
        # Extract version number
        local version=$(echo "$version_output" | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+')
        log "info" "Version check passed" "tool" "$tool" "version" "$version" \
            "output" "$version_output" "phase" "version_check" "result" "pass"
    fi
    
    # Phase 4: Basic functionality test
    log "info" "Running basic functionality test" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "smoke_test"
    local smoke_result smoke_exit
    case "$tool" in
        cass)
            smoke_result=$(cass health 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
        xf)
            smoke_result=$(xf --help 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
        cm)
            smoke_result=$(cm status 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
        ru)
            smoke_result=$(ru --help 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
        ubs)
            smoke_result=$(ubs --help 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
        *)
            smoke_result=$("$tool" --help 2>&1) || smoke_exit=$?
            ;;
    esac
    smoke_exit=${smoke_exit:-0}
    
    if [[ $smoke_exit -ne 0 ]]; then
        log "warn" "Smoke test returned non-zero" "tool" "$tool" "exit_code" "$smoke_exit" \
            "output" "${smoke_result:0:300}" "phase" "smoke_test"
    else
        log "info" "Smoke test passed" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "smoke_test" "result" "pass"
    fi
    
    # Phase 5: Uninstall
    log "info" "Uninstalling tool" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "uninstall"
    local uninstall_output uninstall_exit
    uninstall_output=$(brew uninstall "$tool" 2>&1)
    uninstall_exit=$?
    
    if [[ $uninstall_exit -ne 0 ]]; then
        log "error" "Uninstall failed" "tool" "$tool" "exit_code" "$uninstall_exit" \
            "output" "$uninstall_output" "phase" "uninstall"
        return 1
    fi
    log "info" "Uninstall succeeded" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "uninstall" "result" "pass"
    
    # Phase 6: Verify clean uninstall
    log "info" "Verifying clean uninstall" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "cleanup_check"
    if command -v "$tool" &>/dev/null; then
        log "error" "Tool still in PATH after uninstall" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "cleanup_check"
        return 1
    fi
    log "info" "Clean uninstall verified" "tool" "$tool" "phase" "cleanup_check" "result" "pass"
    
    local end_time=$(date +%s%3N)
    local duration=$((end_time - start_time))
    
    log "info" "Verification complete" "tool" "$tool" "result" "pass" "duration_ms" "$duration"
    return 0
}

# Main execution
log "info" "Starting installation verification suite" "tools" "$TOOLS" "platform" "$(uname -s)"

for tool in $TOOLS; do
    if verify_tool "$tool"; then
        RESULTS+=("$tool:pass")
    else
        RESULTS+=("$tool:fail")
        ((FAILED++))
    fi
done

# Summary
log "info" "Verification suite complete" "total" "${#RESULTS[@]}" "failed" "$FAILED" \
    "results" "$(IFS=,; echo "${RESULTS[*]}")"

exit $FAILED
```

## Script 2: Scoop Installation Verifier

Location: `/data/projects/scoop-bucket/scripts/verify-installation.ps1` (PowerShell)
Location: `/data/projects/scoop-bucket/scripts/verify-installation.sh` (for CI)

The PowerShell version follows the same pattern but uses Scoop commands:
- `scoop install dicklesworthstone/<tool>`
- `<tool> --version`
- `scoop uninstall <tool>`

## Script 3: Cross-Platform CI Workflow

Location: `/data/projects/homebrew-tap/.github/workflows/verify-installation.yml`

```yaml
name: Verify Installation

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * 0'  # Weekly on Sunday
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      tools:
        description: 'Tools to test (comma-separated or "all")'
        default: 'all'

jobs:
  verify-macos-intel:
    runs-on: macos-13  # Intel
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install Homebrew tap
        run: brew tap dicklesworthstone/tap
      - name: Run verification
        run: |
          chmod +x scripts/verify-installation.sh
          LOG_FORMAT=json ./scripts/verify-installation.sh 2>&1 | tee results-macos-intel.json
      - name: Upload results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: results-macos-intel
          path: results-macos-intel.json

  verify-macos-arm:
    runs-on: macos-14  # ARM
    steps:
      # Same as above

  verify-linux:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install Homebrew
        run: |
          /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
          echo 'eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)"' >> ~/.bashrc
          eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
      - name: Install tap and verify
        run: |
          brew tap dicklesworthstone/tap
          LOG_FORMAT=json ./scripts/verify-installation.sh 2>&1 | tee results-linux.json

  verify-windows:
    runs-on: windows-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install Scoop
        run: |
          Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser -Force
          irm get.scoop.sh | iex
      - name: Add bucket and verify
        run: |
          scoop bucket add dicklesworthstone https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/scoop-bucket
          # Run PowerShell verification script
```

## Logging Output Format

All scripts produce JSON logs in this format:

```json
{"timestamp":"2026-01-13T22:30:00Z","level":"info","message":"Starting verification","tool":"cass","phase":"start","platform":"Darwin"}
{"timestamp":"2026-01-13T22:30:01Z","level":"info","message":"Checking formula availability","tool":"cass","phase":"formula_check"}
{"timestamp":"2026-01-13T22:30:02Z","level":"info","message":"Formula found","tool":"cass","phase":"formula_check","result":"pass"}
{"timestamp":"2026-01-13T22:30:03Z","level":"info","message":"Installing tool","tool":"cass","phase":"install"}
{"timestamp":"2026-01-13T22:30:15Z","level":"info","message":"Installation succeeded","tool":"cass","phase":"install","result":"pass","duration_ms":12000}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] verify-installation.sh for Homebrew created and tested
- [ ] verify-installation.ps1 for Scoop created and tested
- [ ] CI workflow runs on all platforms (macOS Intel, macOS ARM, Linux, Windows)
- [ ] JSON logging produces parseable output
- [ ] Scripts handle network failures gracefully
- [ ] Scripts provide clear error messages on failure
- [ ] Results uploaded as CI artifacts
- [ ] Weekly scheduled runs configured
- [ ] Manual dispatch available for testing

## Error Handling Requirements

Scripts must handle and log:
1. Network timeouts during install
2. Missing dependencies
3. Permission errors
4. Tool crashes during smoke test
5. Incomplete uninstallation
